title: Llama7B attention/KV physical HBM and KV-byte-reduction frontier
hypothesis: For llama7b_proxy long-context decode, physically parameterized HBM bandwidth will keep the 131k-token point memory dominated unless KV bytes are structurally reduced through GQA/MQA and packed lower-bit KV storage.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Under plausible physical HBM stack, pseudo-channel, width, MT/s, and efficiency assumptions, which KV sharing and KV bitwidth choices move llama7B 131k decode away from the HBM wall?
include: KV sharing choices MHA, GQA4, GQA8, and MQA
packed KV storage at 16, 8, and 4 bits

expected_benefit: grounds the HBM bound in physical interface parameters instead of bytes per core cycle
tests whether KV-byte reduction is more effective than further scheduler tuning
identifies which KV structure deserves follow-on accuracy and RTL-interface work
risks: HBM efficiency is still an aggregate parameter rather than a DRAM timing trace
KV4 is modeled as packed storage traffic without quality or retraining evidence
SRAM capacity uses an area proxy and should be refined by macro data later

required_evaluations.objective: Run the physical-HBM plus KV-byte-reduction frontier for llama7b_proxy long-context decode.

expected_result.reason: Use the physical HBM and KV-byte-reduction frontier to choose the next structural KV, SRAM, or HBM-interface refinement.
